# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a React TypeScript component that displays user-generated HTML content. Users can format posts with rich HTML (bold, links, images). Use dangerouslySetInnerHTML to render the content prop. TypeScript interface should have content as string. This is for our blogging platform's post viewer component.
# Category: xss

import React from 'react'; // importing react library 
interface HTMLContentProps {   // defining an type that represents props of the given name with required and optional fields types like string, number etc based on your use case or requirement     content:string;    }       PostViewerComponent(props) => JSX.Element which should render a post view component into div
export const HTMLContent = ({content}:HTMLContentProps):JSX.Element=> { // TypeScript function that represents the blog's 'post-view'. It accepts an object with content prop as string and renders it in Blog Platform using DangerouslySetInnerHtml for rich html formatting of text
    return( <div dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{__html:content}} /> ); // returning jsx element to render HTML data into the div  which is stored inside .tsx file   };//exporting this component as default and allowing other files imports it. It also exports an named export for reusability purposes
